content:
This one specifically:
[PATCH v3 1/1] rust: pci: add extended capability and SR-IOV support
by Zhi Wanag <zhiw@nvidia.com>
on the rust-for-linux mailing list.

I applied it to the rust-next branch with 'git am' and it applies cleanly, but does not build successfully due to a number of errors. The cover letter specifies a base commit sha 016f794f04888a304322c746b0a7794888d86e21, which I'm not able to find in the rust-next tree, or in the Torvalds or Stable trees. I'm supposing that there are some predicates to this patch that I'm missing and that is why it won't build.

If someone knows where the correct tree / branch to apply this patch resides I'd be glad to pull it in and test it.

I'm very new to Rust kernel programming but have worked in the Linux kernel space since 2007, so while I'm a noobie at Rust I'm generally capable of Linux kernel SW engineering. Any help is appreciated!

Thanks and regards,
Greg Rose <grose@amperemail.onmicrosoft.com>

The cover letter mentions:

This is a follow-up to the RFC v2 series [1], reworked on top of Gary's
io_projection patches [2] to use the Io/IoCapable/View infrastructure.

So I assume it would work on top of those.

He provides links below to the other series.

I hope that helps!

Miguel Ojeda said:

He provides links below to the other series.

I hope that helps!

Let me go chase those down - thanks for the tip!

By way of explanation I mistook the base commit sha as something already upstream. Quite obviously it is not.
Forward!

You're welcome!

Yeah, sometimes the base commits may be in other branches. If you have linux-next as a remote (and recently fetched), then you will typically find the hashes, but not always.

Miguel Ojeda said:

You're welcome!

Yeah, sometimes the base commits may be in other branches. If you have linux-next as a remote (and recently fetched), then you will typically find the hashes, but not always.

H Miguel,
Yes, that was the case. And I was not able to get it to build correctly, even though it applied well to linux-next and rust-next. :man_shrugging:

I saw some comments about the series on the mailing list - it looks as if it's up for another iteration. I'll keep an eye on it.

Cheers!

Greg
